title: Teleportation Protocol Demo
description: Implement the standard quantum teleportation protocol and explain why it requires both entanglement and a classical channel.
whyItMatters: Teleportation is one of the clearest examples of quantum information engineering. It links state transfer, entanglement, measurement, and networking in one compact protocol.

steps.title: Prepare the input and Bell pair
steps.description: Set an arbitrary single-qubit state as the payload and generate a shared entangled pair.
steps.tips: Test with a few known input states before generalising.

steps.title: Apply the sender-side operations
steps.description: Entangle the payload with one half of the Bell pair and measure the sender qubits.
steps.tips: Track which classical bits are produced and what they mean.

steps.title: Apply receiver corrections
steps.description: Use the classical bits to choose the appropriate correction on the receiver qubit.
steps.tips: This is the step that proves no faster-than-light shortcut is happening.

steps.title: Verify state recovery
steps.description: Compare the output state to the original input across several examples.
steps.tips: Use fidelity or statevector comparison if available.

steps.title: Explain the resource logic
steps.description: Write a short note on what the entanglement does and what the classical bits do.
steps.tips: Good explanations separate correlation resource from control instruction.

deliverables: Verification results for multiple input states
Short explanation of entanglement plus classical communication

rubric.description: The circuit implements teleportation faithfully and reconstructs the state.
The learner shows evidence that the output matches the intended input.
The explanation avoids common misconceptions about teleportation.
